title: FPP pluginInfo.json
description: Metadata file every FPP plugin ships in its repository root. Prose reference: PLUGININFO_FORMAT.md in this repo. FPP ignores unknown top-level keys, so this schema is intentionally lenient (additionalProperties allowed) and validates the keys FPP actually reads.

repoName.description: Unique identifier and on-disk install directory name. Keep it filesystem/shell safe. Must match the name in fpp-data/pluginList.json.

name.description: Human-readable title shown on the plugin card.

description.description: Short description shown on the plugin card.

homeURL.description: Project home page; rendered as the home link.

srcURL.description: Git clone URL (normally ending in .git). FPP clones this to install.

bugURL.description: Issue tracker URL; rendered as Report a Bug.

iconURL.description: Icon URL (PNG, 128x128 or 256x256). FPP prefers a local icon.png in the repo root and falls back to this.

allowUpdates.description: 0 hides in-place update buttons (install-once plugins). Omitted = allowed. Can be overridden per version.

minMemoryMB.description: Default minimum system RAM (MB) across all versions. Below this, FPP flags the plugin and hides it on the Basic UI level. Can be overridden per version.

minCpuCores.description: Default minimum recommended CPU cores across all versions. Below this, FPP flags the plugin and hides it on the Basic UI level. Can be overridden per version.

private.description: true if hosted in a private GitHub repo; installed with the credentials on the Developer settings page.

linkName.description: Legacy: creates a symlink on install. Only takes effect when the cloned repo does NOT ship its own pluginInfo.json. New plugins rarely need it.

delist.description: Set true to request removal from FPP's Plugin Manager list (retire the plugin). Because setting it requires write access to your repo, it also serves as proof of ownership for a de-list request. Existing installs are unaffected.

versions.description: Compatibility entries. FPP installs the first entry whose FPP-version range and platform match the device.

dependencies.description: Things installed automatically BEFORE the plugin's own scripts/fpp_install.sh (FPP 10+). Must be TOP-LEVEL; FPP does not read a dependencies block nested inside a versions[] entry.

platform.description: Must exactly match the value FPP writes to /etc/fpp/platform.

minFPPVersion.description: Minimum FPP version this entry supports, e.g. "9.0".

maxFPPVersion.description: Maximum FPP version. "0", "0.0" or "" mean open-ended (valid through the rest of the current major series).

branch.description: Git branch to check out for this entry.

sha.description: Commit to pin to. "" installs the latest commit on branch.

allowUpdates.description: Per-version override of the top-level allowUpdates.

platforms.description: Restrict this entry to specific hardware. Omit to match all platforms.

dependencies.description: Resolved in order: packages -> scripts -> plugins.

packages.description: System (apt) packages. Reference-counted per requester. Needs a Debian-family platform.

plugins.description: Other FPP plugins by repoName. Each must exist in fpp-data/pluginList.json.
